title: Grammar — Modal Verbs (My Next Grammar, Lesson 14)
intro: Modal verbs are helping verbs like can, could, may, might, must, should, and would. They tell us about ability, permission, possibility, advice, or what we have to do.

items.q: I ____ swim very well. I have practiced for years.
____ I borrow your pencil, please?
It looks cloudy. It ____ rain later.
You ____ wear a helmet when you ride a bike. It's the rule.
You look tired. You ____ go to bed early tonight.
When I was little, I ____ climb that tree easily.
____ you like some apple juice?
Students ____ not run in the hallway.
I'm not sure where Sam is. He ____ be at the library.
You ____ brush your teeth before bed.
The baby is only one year old. She ____ talk yet.
____ you please close the window?
He is the best player on the team. He ____ practice a lot.
We ____ go to the park if the weather is nice.
If I had wings, I ____ fly to the moon.
You ____ not eat my lunch without asking.
Birds ____ build nests with twigs and grass.
It's getting dark. We ____ go home now.
____ I help you carry that bag?
She ____ speak three languages: English, Chinese, and Spanish.
